message:
feat(workflows/runner): tick 190 handle_detection_with_cx inlines body + routes start_with_id_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

handle_detection_with_cx previously only pre-flight-checked cx via
is_cancel_requested() and then delegated to the ambient-cx
handle_detection. The inner engine.start_with_id call was therefore an
orphan-cx hole — a detection dispatched to a healthy cx could block
on a backpressured storage write past a later cancellation.

This tick inlines the detection body (find_matching_workflow → lock
acquisition → context build → engine start) so we can route the
engine start through engine.start_with_id_cx(cx, ...). That method,
thanks to tick 189, now threads cx into storage.upsert_workflow_with_cx
— so a cancelled caller releases at the storage pre-flight checkpoint
or the mpsc backpressure reserve, not after the write lands.

No behavior change to detection matching, lock acquisition, or context
payload. Legacy `handle_detection` preserved verbatim for non-asupersync
builds and for ambient-cx callers.

Verification: 1168 / 1168 workflows:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass.

Cumulative orphan-cx progress in workflows/:
- run_workflow_inner strangler-fig: complete (ticks 178-187)
- WorkflowEngine cx-first helpers: 6/6 (ticks 188-189)
- handle_detection_with_cx: cx-threaded end-to-end ✅ (this)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
